ESG Knowledge Graph

Fortune 500 ESG Knowledge Graph

This site presents my ESG knowledge graph built from public Fortune 500 annual reports, sustainability reports, and carbon-related disclosures. The public-facing version now centers on 351 published companies, while another 147 remain outside the formal graph because a parent-company report is missing or the match still requires review.

The main figure sequence now follows the stakeholder's requested logic more directly: standards layering, GHG or ISO or GB chains, scope and direct or indirect emissions, methodology and data quality, activity and industry mapping, and technology clustering. The public pages now retain only formal world500 results.

Download published GraphMLDownload published nodes.csvDownload published edges.csv

Two priority axes on the homepage

Standards and Frameworks Layer

Identifies whether firms follow GHG, ISO, GB/T, GRI, TCFD, IFRS S2, ESRS/CSRD and related systems, then classifies them by standard, guideline, principle, assurance standard, and target-validation role.

Emissions Methodology and Data Quality Layer

Identifies primary data, secondary data, estimation/derivation, report disclosure, verification, assurance, validation, and activity classes such as stationary, mobile, fugitive, process, purchased electricity, and Scope 3 categories.

Current Status Distribution Across 500 Companies

This section summarizes, under the final public scope, which of the 500 companies are already included in the formal graph and why the remainder are still outside it, closing exactly to the full 500-company universe.

Status summary

351 companies are already included in the formal graph and 149 are still outside it; most of the remaining gap is caused by missing parent-company main reports in the current corpus.

Included in formal graph351
Currently outside formal graph149

Why companies remain outside the formal graph

The remaining gap is driven primarily by missing parent-company main reports rather than by unresolved alias normalization.

Affiliate-only files found6

Formal Knowledge Graph Overview

This section shows a readable structural backbone plus full graph statistics instead of a dense node cloud. The formal graph connects companies, reports, standards, activity classes, scope, data-quality methods, and industry sections in one integrated structure.

Standards Entity Navigator: Drill Down from GHG / ISO / GB to Specific Standards and Companies

This workspace merges the three separate entity chains into one navigable entity-level knowledge-graph console. The reading order is fixed as system first, then specific standard, then company, directly matching the stakeholder's requested standards axis.

The right panel synchronizes company profile, standard roles and principles, and the full entity graph for the current system; the lower panels turn the current view into a briefing table and compact knowledge-base summary.

GHG / ISO / GB Entity-level Knowledge-graph Overview

These three figures show the entity-level knowledge graphs for the 351 published companies under GHG, ISO, and GB systems respectively, so the site no longer appears to expose only the default GHG view.

The full entity graph for the selected system stays available here, while the left workspace emphasizes readable drill-down and company filtering.

Method and Data-Quality Entity Navigator: Drill Down from Method Dimension to Category and Company

This second axis is also rendered as an entity-level knowledge-graph workspace. The reading order is fixed as method dimension first, then specific category, then company, directly matching the stakeholder's focus on data quality, activity classes, and methodological basis.

The right panel synchronizes scope coverage, direct or indirect emissions, boundary basis, and estimate basis for the selected company; the lower panels turn the current view into a briefing table and compact knowledge-base summary.

Figure 1 to Figure 6 Formal Briefing Chain and Structured Extracts

These six primary figures now follow a briefing-outline structure, moving from the standards axis to the emissions-boundary axis, method and data-quality axis, activity and industry axis, and technology-path axis. The 351-versus-147 completion boundary is stated in the status section above.

Figure 1

Figure 1 Standards Axis I: GHG / ISO / GB Layers and Supporting Frameworks

Briefing takeaway: This opening figure answers which standards systems firms primarily follow, foregrounding GHG / ISO / GB first and then the supporting disclosure and target frameworks.
Footer Note: Scope: standards facts for 351 published companies. Layout: the left panel foregrounds GHG / ISO / GB systems before supporting frameworks, and the right panel shows frequent specific standards and frameworks. Use: answers which systems and frameworks firms cite.

Current graph logic

Standards are modeled separately from standard-reference facts, so one framework can play different roles across reports.

Emission values are modeled separately from methodology facts, allowing each scope record to connect to data source, assurance stage, activity category, and calculation method.

Industry classification follows GB/T 4754-2017 at the section level to maintain a consistent cross-company comparison basis.

Activity tagging distinguishes explicit mentions from evidence-based mapping to support later human review and reinsertion.

This site contains presentation content only, not source code or conversation history.
